steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text introduces ancient DNA (the established approach), then pivots with "However" to Dussex et al.'s use of historical DNA from museum specimens, ending with "thus capitalizing on the research potential" of that resource. The structure question asks which choice captures this two-approach shape and the emphasis on promise.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'compares two methods...then explains why the second is underused': the text does not explain why historical DNA is underused; it emphasizes its potential. ✗
  • Choice B — 'describes two approaches...then emphasizes how promising one is': ancient DNA (approach 1) then historical DNA (approach 2), with the closing phrase stressing its promise. Matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'summarizes previous research...then shows scientists challenge its findings': the Dussex study uses a different method, but it does not challenge or contradict prior research findings. ✗
  • Choice D — 'provides an overview...then asserts the alternative is superior': the text never claims historical DNA is superior to ancient DNA, only that it has untapped potential. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
